title: Knight Platformer
cardSummary: A 2D browser-playable platformer built in Godot as a hands-on game dev learning exercise.
summary: A 2D platformer built in Godot to explore game development fundamentals — physics, movement, and level design — playable directly in the browser.
description: Knight Platformer was a self-directed project to get hands-on experience with game development. Building it in Godot meant learning its scene system, physics engine, and GDScript while designing something actually fun to play.

The game runs entirely in the browser with smooth character movement using arrow keys or WASD, and a jump mechanic bound to Space. Shipping it as a web export made it easy to share without requiring any installation.
highlights: Playable in the browser via Godot's HTML5 export
Supports keyboard controls (WASD / arrow keys + Space)
Built as a learning exercise in 2D game design and Godot's engine

title: Embedded Board (FPGA) Wave Analyzer
cardSummary: Real-time analysis and visualization of FPGA signals via UART.
summary: A desktop tool for real-time inspection and visualization of FPGA signals to make embedded debugging faster and more intuitive.
description: This project focused on improving the debugging experience for embedded systems work. The application receives signal data over UART and presents it in a more readable, interactive format.
It was especially useful for validating FPGA behavior during development, while also giving the user a cleaner workflow than raw serial output.
highlights: Visualizes FPGA signals in real time
Built as a desktop GUI for debugging workflows

title: Financial Budget Simulator
cardSummary: User-friendly GUI for data analysis and financial forecasting.
summary: A GUI application for scenario planning, budgeting, and financial forecasting with a focus on clarity for non-technical users.
description: I built this tool to turn financial analysis into a more interactive workflow. Instead of relying on static spreadsheets, users could test scenarios and inspect how assumptions affected projected outcomes.
The emphasis was on usability and clear visual feedback so that the tool could support practical decision-making.

title: Swarmbot
summary: A robotics project exploring wireless communication and coordination between small Arduino-based robots.
description: Swarmbot explored how simple robots could exchange information and coordinate behavior over Wi-Fi. The project combined embedded programming, communication logic, and hands-on hardware integration.
It was a good example of working across both software and electronics to create a more dynamic robotics system.

title: Energy Usage Predictor
cardSummary: Machine learning project that predicts household energy usage.
summary: A machine learning project for modeling and predicting household energy consumption from historical usage patterns.
description: This project focused on forecasting residential energy usage using historical data and feature engineering. The goal was to understand how well a predictive model could capture consumption trends over time.
It was a strong end-to-end exercise in dataset preparation, model experimentation, and communicating results.
highlights: Explored feature engineering for forecast quality

title: Minigame Board
cardSummary: Chrome's Dinosaur Game implemented in an FPGA.
summary: An FPGA implementation of a familiar arcade-style game, combining digital design with real-time interaction.
description: This project recreated a simple game experience directly in hardware, which made it a fun way to apply digital logic concepts in a visible, interactive format.
It required translating gameplay behavior into hardware-oriented thinking, including timing, input handling, and display logic.
highlights: Implemented game logic on FPGA hardware
Applied digital systems concepts in practice

title: PDF Edit
cardSummary: Python package for automating workflows in PDFs.
summary: A lightweight Python package for automating repetitive PDF editing and document processing tasks.
description: I built PDF Edit to simplify PDF-heavy workflows that usually take a lot of manual effort. The goal was to make repetitive document operations scriptable and dependable.
The project is structured as a reusable Python package, which makes it easy to integrate into larger data or operations pipelines.
highlights: Packaged for reuse in different workflows
Designed to reduce manual document handling time

title: Robotic Hand
cardSummary: A robotics build focused on control, sensing, and physical interaction.
summary: A robotics project centered on actuation, control, and building a physical system that can translate software logic into motion.
description: The robotic hand project focused on connecting hardware control with real-world movement. Projects like this are especially valuable because they force software decisions to account for physical constraints and system behavior.
It combined experimentation in mechanics, embedded control, and iterative testing.
highlights: Worked across software and hardware boundaries
Focused on actuation and physical control
Iterated through testing on a real device

title: Web Scraping
cardSummary: A program that acquires product data from ecommerce websites.
summary: A data collection tool for extracting structured product information from ecommerce sites for downstream analysis.
description: This project automated product-data collection from ecommerce websites so the results could be analyzed at scale instead of gathered manually.
The main challenge was building a workflow that was reliable enough for repeated use and useful for pricing or market analysis.

title: Image Processing Bot
cardSummary: A Raspberry Pi robot that can follow objects using image processing.
summary: A robotics project that combined computer vision and embedded hardware to let a robot track and follow objects.
description: This was one of my earlier projects combining software, electronics, and image processing. The robot used a Raspberry Pi-based setup to interpret visual input and react to what it detected.
It was an important foundation for later work in robotics because it connected perception, decision-making, and motion in one system.
highlights: Used image processing for object tracking
